title: Verifiable ad delivery and influencer metrics
problem: Ad networks report impressions and clicks using their own unauditable metrics, enabling widespread fraud. Brands also pay influencers based on self-reported or platform-reported reach and engagement that is trivially inflated through bots and fake followers.
solution: Transparent impression tracking and verifiable reach metrics — with optional zero-knowledge proofs for audience size — so advertisers can independently confirm that spend reached real human audiences.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: delivery events and engagement signals can be cryptographically attested — giving advertisers independent proof that their spend reached real audiences without relying on the platform's own reporting.

title: Affordable housing covenant enforcement
problem: Affordable housing deed restrictions — requiring units to remain below market rate for 30, 50, or 99 years — are written into paper legal documents that must be manually checked at every ownership transfer, and routinely slip through conveyancing processes, effectively converting affordable units to market rate with no enforcement mechanism.
solution: Affordability restrictions encoded directly into property tokens that enforce covenants automatically at the point of transfer or rental — making it structurally impossible to sell or rent above the allowed price without the covenant being triggered.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: affordability rules are baked into the asset itself and execute at every transfer — covenant compliance becomes a property of the token, not a legal obligation that requires someone to notice and litigate a violation.

title: Age and eligibility proofs without identity disclosure
problem: A teenager trying to prove they're 18 to access a website, or a doctor proving they hold a medical license to prescribe remotely, must hand over their full government ID — exposing date of birth, address, and ID number to a third party who only needed a yes/no answer — creating surveillance infrastructure and breach risk for a simple eligibility check.
solution: Zero-knowledge proofs that let individuals demonstrate they meet a threshold — over 18, licensed physician, accredited investor — without revealing the underlying document, name, or any other personal attribute beyond the specific claim being verified.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: zero-knowledge proofs confirm eligibility cryptographically without exposing underlying identity — platforms get regulatory compliance without building a database of sensitive documents that becomes a breach target.

title: Agent-to-agent commerce protocols
problem: As specialized AI agents proliferate — managing calendars, researching suppliers, executing purchases — they need to hire, pay, and contract with each other autonomously. AI shopping agents also have no standardized way to hold funds or build verifiable transaction histories across merchants. There is no open protocol for agents to discover services, agree terms, and settle payments without routing every transaction through a human approval loop.
solution: Open protocols for AI agents to publish capabilities, negotiate service terms, execute purchases, and settle micropayments autonomously — creating a machine-to-machine service economy where agents from any organization can transact as fluidly as APIs call each other today.
whyEthereum: Composability: a shared settlement layer with open standards lets agents from any organization transact without pre-negotiated bilateral integrations — the agent economy becomes interoperable by default rather than requiring every agent developer to build custom payment bridges.

title: Economic identity for agents and devices
problem: Autonomous AI agents and IoT devices interact with services and execute transactions but have no standardized identity, wallet, or payment rails — making it impossible to attribute actions, manage permissions, hold assets, or enable machine-scale micropayments without routing every transaction through human-operated intermediaries. IoT devices face the same gap at the infrastructure level: they depend on centralized certificate authorities for identity, creating single points of failure and making ownership transfer cumbersome.
solution: Onchain identity and payment rails purpose-built for autonomous agents and devices — enabling them to hold funds, pay for services, sign transactions, transfer ownership, and build verifiable track records across platforms without human approval loops or centralized registries.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: smart contracts can scope an agent's permissions and spending limits programmatically, and Ethereum's global settlement layer means an agent's identity and funds work across any application without custom integrations.

title: Tamper-evident maintenance records for aviation MRO
problem: Airlines and MRO providers maintain aircraft maintenance records across disconnected paper logbooks and legacy systems, making FAA/EASA audit preparation weeks of manual work and leaving no reliable way to detect falsified inspection signoffs — a safety failure that has contributed to multiple airworthiness directives and enforcement actions.
solution: Immutable, per-component maintenance logs that record every inspection, part replacement, and airworthiness directive compliance event — giving regulators a continuous, tamper-evident audit trail rather than sampling paper records during periodic inspections.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: each maintenance event is cryptographically attested at the time it occurs — falsified inspections become detectable after the fact, and audit preparation collapses from weeks to hours because the regulator reads the same ledger the MRO writes to.

title: Business registries and beneficial ownership
problem: Verifying a company's registration, licensing status, or true ownership requires navigating multiple disconnected databases — and existing beneficial ownership registries rely on self-reported filings with no verification mechanism, allowing shell company structures to hide the real owners of assets across jurisdictions. This structural opacity is systematically exploited for money laundering, sanctions evasion, and tax fraud.
solution: Cryptographically attested business registration and beneficial ownership records that chain legal entities to verified ultimate owners across jurisdictions — enabling instant verification for regulators, banks, and counterparties without relying on self-filed declarations or bilateral data-sharing between registries.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: ownership attestations are cryptographically signed and immutable across jurisdictions — false filings become detectable and cross-border verification happens on a shared neutral ledger rather than through slow bilateral regulatory cooperation.

title: Shared-ledger bookkeeping with counterparty confirmation
problem: Intercompany transactions and multi-entity consolidation require each party to independently record and reconcile the same economic event — a process that consumes 30-40% of the time in monthly close cycles and makes audit preparation expensive because auditors must independently confirm balances with counterparties through a manual confirmation process that takes weeks.
solution: A shared ledger where both counterparties to a transaction confirm the same record at the point of origination — eliminating the reconciliation step during close and giving auditors real-time access to pre-confirmed balances rather than requiring them to send confirmation letters and wait for responses.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: every transaction is confirmed by both counterparties at origination on a shared, tamper-evident ledger — the monthly reconciliation step that drives close cycle length and audit cost is eliminated by construction.

whyEthereum: Enforcement: membership transfers, governance votes, and financial distributions execute within rules encoded once in the smart contract — reducing per-unit administrative cost without requiring the organization to abandon its existing legal structure or tax-exempt status.

title: Transparent community space revenue and allocation
problem: Revenue from commercial use of public spaces — parks, plazas, community halls — flows into general municipal budgets where residents can't see how much their neighborhood's spaces generated or how those funds were spent. Allocation decisions happen in opaque committee processes with no audit trail, and community groups that maintain or improve a space have no formal mechanism to influence how it's governed.
solution: A transparent treasury for each community space where commercial booking revenue is publicly tracked and spending decisions are made through participatory governance by verified local residents — creating a direct feedback loop between a space's economic activity and its neighborhood's investment priorities.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: every booking payment and treasury disbursement is publicly auditable — residents can see exactly how much revenue their neighborhood's spaces generate and whether spending decisions reflect community priorities.

title: Privacy-preserving KYC and sanctions screening proofs
problem: Every financial institution that onboards the same customer independently collects and stores the same identity documents, running redundant KYC checks that cost $50–500 per customer — while creating dozens of copies of sensitive identity data across institutions that each become breach targets. Cross-border transactions compound the problem: a European bank processing a payment to a US correspondent must demonstrate OFAC and EU sanctions compliance, which currently requires sharing enough customer data for the counterparty to independently screen — forcing a choice between privacy and compliance.
solution: Zero-knowledge proofs that let institutions demonstrate a customer passed KYC and sanctions screening — proving the fact of compliance without sharing the underlying identity documents, so counterparties can satisfy their regulatory obligations without receiving (and storing) sensitive data they didn't need.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: ZK proofs confirm compliance status cryptographically — counterparties verify that screening was performed without receiving the underlying identity data, reducing breach surface while satisfying correspondent banking compliance requirements.

title: Automated agricultural subsidy disbursement
problem: Agricultural subsidy programs (e.g., EU Common Agricultural Policy direct payments, USDA conservation programs) require farmers to submit annual applications, undergo eligibility reviews, and wait months for payment — a process where administrative overhead consumes a significant share of program budgets and delays reach the farmers who need operating capital most. Eligibility conditions like acreage verification, crop compliance, and conservation practice adoption are checked manually by inspectors who can physically visit only a fraction of enrolled farms each year.
solution: Subsidy disbursements that execute automatically when satellite-verified acreage data, crop identification, and conservation practice adoption confirm eligibility — reducing administrative costs and getting payments to farmers at the start of the season rather than months after application.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: payments release automatically when oracle-verified eligibility conditions are confirmed — farmers receive funds when they need operating capital rather than after months of manual review, and the eligibility check is continuous rather than sampled.

title: Content provenance and authenticity
problem: There is no widespread mechanism to verify who created a post, photo, video, or article — making impersonation trivial and deepfake attribution impossible to contest. The problem compounds with AI: AI-generated content is indistinguishable from human-created content once shared, and users receiving AI outputs have no way to verify which model produced a given result, enabling manipulation and undermining accountability for both human and AI-generated misinformation.
solution: Signed posts, media receipts, and provenance chains that cover both human-created and AI-generated content — cryptographically binding content to its origin at creation time, so that deepfakes, impersonation, and model-version manipulation are publicly detectable.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: cryptographic signatures attached at creation time prove who made content and when — impersonation and retroactive denial become publicly detectable rather than just deniable.

title: Decentralized insurance claims adjudication
problem: Insurance companies face hundreds of disputed claims monthly that don't fit automated processing rules — ambiguous coverage questions, contested damage assessments, conflicting evidence — and route them through traditional arbitration that takes 7–10 days, costs hundreds of dollars per case, and produces outcomes neither party finds credible.
solution: Specialized juror pools that evaluate disputed insurance claims using standardized evidence packages and structured deliberation protocols — with jurors staking tokens on their verdicts, outcomes determined by stake-weighted consensus, and payment executing automatically once adjudication is complete.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: adjudication outcomes execute automatically via smart contract — neither the insurer nor the claimant can delay compliance with a verdict, and the juror incentive structure is enforced programmatically rather than by a professional arbitration body.

title: Tamper-evident data integrity for decentralized clinical trials
problem: Decentralized clinical trials (DCTs) — where patients participate from home using wearables, ePRO apps, and telemedicine — are growing rapidly but face a data integrity challenge that traditional site-based trials don't: when data is collected on patient devices across hundreds of locations rather than at monitored investigator sites, sponsors and regulators have weaker assurance that electronic source data hasn't been altered, that consent was properly obtained, or that the chain of custody from device to database is intact. FDA's 2023 DCT guidance specifically calls out electronic source data integrity as a key concern.
solution: Cryptographic attestation of clinical trial data at the point of collection — patient-reported outcomes, wearable sensor readings, and eConsent signatures are hashed and timestamped on an immutable ledger at the moment of capture, creating an audit trail that satisfies 21 CFR Part 11 requirements and gives monitors, sponsors, and regulators confidence in source data integrity without requiring physical site visits.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: trial data is cryptographically committed at the point of collection — monitors and regulators can verify source data integrity without physical site visits, satisfying the data provenance requirements that make regulators hesitant to approve decentralized trial designs.

title: Instant music sync licensing
problem: A filmmaker, YouTuber, or advertiser who wants to license a song for their production must either navigate months-long negotiations with major labels — who often decline small requests entirely — or use generic royalty-free music, because there's no scalable way for independent artists to make their catalog licensable at standardized rates.

whyEthereum: Enforcement: the license purchase and rights holder payment execute atomically in a single transaction — the buyer gets the license the moment they pay, and every co-writer and producer receives their split without a publishing administrator in the middle.

title: Decision markets for corporate governance
problem: Corporate decisions — which strategy to pursue, whether to hire or fire an executive, which product to prioritize — are made through a combination of politics, hierarchy, and intuition rather than mechanisms that aggregate genuine beliefs of those closest to the work. Board votes and management sign-off concentrate decision-making power in ways that reward confidence and seniority over accuracy, with no feedback loop that punishes bad predictions or rewards good ones. Organizations systematically act on what's good for those in power rather than what's good for the organization.
solution: Conditional prediction markets tied to company-specific metrics — share price, TVL, revenue, retention — where any proposal generates two parallel markets: expected metric outcome if it passes, and if it fails. The proposal executes if markets predict a positive impact. Participants with genuine information have financial incentive to trade accurately. Early implementations include Butter Markets (used in Optimism's grant allocation experiments) and MetaDAO, which has run 96+ proposals for 14 organizations using this mechanism.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: conditional markets execute and settle via smart contract, making the link between market signal and organizational decision a protocol property rather than management discretion. Composability: the same decision market infrastructure can be referenced by any organization's governance without rebuilding from scratch.

title: DER aggregation and behind-the-meter market participation
problem: FERC Order 2222 requires ISOs/RTOs to let distributed energy resources — residential batteries, smart thermostats, EV chargers, water heaters — participate in wholesale energy markets through aggregators. But the settlement infrastructure doesn't exist: aggregators have no scalable way to meter, verify, and compensate individual behind-the-meter assets for the load flexibility they provide, because each device is on a different protocol and the minimum transaction size in wholesale markets makes sub-kWh contributions uneconomic to settle individually.
solution: A settlement layer for DER aggregation that meters individual device contributions, verifies demand reductions against smart meter baselines, and distributes compensation to device owners automatically — making it economically viable for aggregators to orchestrate millions of small assets into wholesale market participation.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: compensation for individual device contributions executes automatically when metered demand reductions are confirmed — enabling aggregators to settle millions of micro-transactions that would be uneconomic through traditional payment infrastructure.

title: Grid-edge device coordination and tamper-evident event logging
problem: Distribution utilities adding thousands of DERs, smart inverters, and storage systems to the grid edge have no unified view of device state or energy flows below the substation level — SCADA systems designed for centralized generation don't extend to the grid edge, and the growing attack surface of connected devices makes it critical to maintain tamper-evident records of device commands and state changes for NERC CIP compliance and incident forensics.
solution: An append-only event log for grid-edge device actions — recording commands, state changes, and energy flow measurements with cryptographic attestation — giving distribution operators and regulators a tamper-evident audit trail at the grid edge where SCADA visibility currently ends.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: device actions and energy flow data are cryptographically logged — giving distribution operators an audit trail below the substation level that SCADA systems don't provide, and making unauthorized device commands forensically detectable.

title: Esports & leagues
problem: Esports tournaments rely on centralized organizers for brackets, anti-cheat enforcement, and prize distribution, introducing disputes over fairness, delayed payouts, and opaque decision-making.
solution: Transparent tournament brackets, provable anti-cheat attestations, instant rule-based payouts reduce overhead and disputes

title: Cross-network EV charging roaming settlement
problem: When an EV driver charges on a network other than their home provider, the roaming transaction passes through bilateral settlement agreements between the charge point operator (CPO) and the eMobility service provider (eMSP) — each pair requiring a custom integration. In Europe alone there are 500+ CPOs and dozens of eMSPs, and the bilateral model means most roaming pairs simply don't exist, leaving drivers unable to charge on large portions of the network without creating new accounts.
solution: A shared settlement layer where any CPO and any eMSP can clear roaming transactions without bilateral agreements — using standardized session records (kWh delivered, time, location, tariff) that both parties confirm onchain, with payment executing automatically at session close.
whyEthereum: Composability: a shared settlement layer means any CPO-eMSP pair can transact without a pre-negotiated bilateral integration — roaming coverage expands from bilateral partnerships to the entire network by default.

title: Cross-jurisdictional fisheries quota tracking
problem: Regional fisheries management organizations (RFMOs) allocate catch quotas across member states, but enforcement depends on self-reported landing declarations and port state inspections that cover a fraction of actual landings. Vessels quota-hop between jurisdictions, and IUU (illegal, unreported, unregulated) fishing accounts for up to 26 million tonnes annually — partly because no RFMO can see another's allocation and catch data in real time.
solution: A shared catch accounting ledger across RFMOs and port states where landing declarations, VMS positions, and observer reports are recorded against vessel-level quota allocations — making overfishing and quota-hopping detectable across jurisdictional boundaries without relying on bilateral data-sharing requests that take weeks.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: catch events from multiple jurisdictions are recorded on a neutral shared ledger — overfishing by a vessel quota-hopping across RFMOs becomes publicly detectable rather than hidden in incompatible national reporting systems.

title: Food provenance and traceability
problem: When a food safety recall is issued, tracing contaminated product back to the source takes days or weeks because lot IDs change at every handoff between grower, packer, distributor, and retailer — each maintaining their own disconnected records — meaning contaminated product stays on shelves while investigators manually reconstruct the chain.
solution: A shared lot ID standard that persists immutably through every handoff in the supply chain — so when a retailer scans a product, they can trace it back to the specific farm field and harvest date in seconds, and a recall can be executed against a precise subset of product rather than pulling entire categories from shelves.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: each handoff in the supply chain is recorded as an immutable event — making recalls faster to execute and food fraud detectable by anyone with access to the chain.

title: Futarchy for public policy
problem: Democratic systems are good at aggregating what citizens want but systematically bad at determining which policies will actually deliver it. Voters choose representatives based on stated intentions; representatives vote on bills based on lobbying, ideology, and coalition politics rather than evidence of likely impact. Expert panels and think tanks aggregate some information but lack skin in the game — a consultant who recommends a bad policy faces no financial consequence.
solution: A governance layer that separates the question of values (decided democratically: what should we optimize for?) from the question of means (decided by markets: which policy will best achieve it?). Elected representatives define a measurable welfare metric — GDP, life expectancy, emissions index — and conditional prediction markets determine which policies are expected to raise it. A policy becomes law only when market estimates clearly predict it will improve welfare relative to the status quo. Robin Hanson's original formulation ('vote on values, bet on beliefs') has been live-tested by MetaDAO for DAO governance and explored by at least one government health agency since 2024.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: the link between market outcome and policy adoption executes automatically, removing the possibility of political override after the market has spoken. Verifiability: welfare metric measurement and market prices are publicly auditable, making it impossible to quietly override the market signal without detection.

title: Privacy-preserving firearms provenance for voluntary transfer verification
problem: Private firearms sales in most US states have no mechanism for buyers to verify that a weapon wasn't stolen and no way for sellers to confirm a buyer isn't prohibited — a gap that exists partly because any centralized registry is politically untenable (the Tiahrt Amendment prohibits a searchable federal database) and partly because existing background check infrastructure (NICS) is unavailable for private sales in most states. The result is that stolen firearms circulate in secondary markets with no provenance trail.
solution: A voluntary, privacy-preserving provenance system where firearm transfers are recorded as encrypted attestations — a buyer can verify a weapon isn't flagged as stolen and a seller can confirm the buyer passed a background check, without either party's identity being stored in a searchable registry or accessible to government without a warrant.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: ZK proofs confirm provenance and background check status without creating a searchable ownership database — enabling voluntary compliance for private sales without triggering the political opposition that blocks centralized registry proposals.

title: Harvest pre-financing for smallholder farmers
problem: A subsistence farmer who needs $300 to buy seeds and fertilizer at planting time has no collateral for a bank loan and no trading history with formal lenders — so they borrow from local moneylenders at 60–100% annual interest rates, or go without inputs and accept a lower yield, trapping them in a cycle where they can never accumulate enough surplus to escape the next season's dependency.
solution: Pre-harvest financing backed by tokenized future crop receipts — letting farmers collateralize their standing crop against verified planting data, weather feeds, and historical yield records, with repayment automatically deducted from the proceeds when the harvest is sold.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: repayment executes automatically from harvest sale proceeds when the crop is sold — lenders can extend credit against verifiable onchain collateral rather than paper promises, and farmers don't need to make a separate manual repayment.

title: Hyperstition markets
problem: Many valuable outcomes — a new open standard getting adopted, a local currency achieving network effects, a civic technology deployed across a city — require enough people to act simultaneously that no individual will move first. Traditional crowdfunding pools money for single projects but can't coordinate the distributed, multi-actor contributions needed for an ecosystem to tip. Prediction markets aggregate beliefs about what will happen but don't actively create the conditions for it.
solution: Markets designed not just to predict outcomes but to manifest them — where buying YES stakes participants financially in making a prediction true, and where public prices function as Schelling points that coordinate action around shared futures. As more participants buy YES, the rising price signals growing collective commitment, rewarding early believers who do the hard coordination work more than late arrivals. Applications include bootstrapping open-source ecosystems, accelerating civic tech adoption, and creating self-fulfilling network effects for new standards. Note: Vitalik Buterin has flagged meaningful manipulation risks — specifically the 'hitman problem' where actors profit from causing bad outcomes, and whale concentration enabling engineered rather than emergent coordination.
whyEthereum: Composability: hyperstition markets can compose with attestation systems, identity layers, and impact certificates so that demonstrated contributions — not just financial bets — are verifiable and rewarded. Enforcement: payouts to early believers execute automatically when resolution conditions are met, without a platform operator deciding who contributed enough.

title: Impact certificates with retroactive outcome verification
problem: Social-impact funders have no reliable way to verify that grant-funded programs delivered promised outcomes — a job training program reports 80% placement rates, but the funder has no independent mechanism to confirm these numbers, and the incentive structure rewards narrative over evidence because future grants depend on reported (not verified) impact.
solution: Tokenized impact certificates that represent verified outcomes — issued when an independent evaluator confirms results (e.g., verified job placements, measured emissions reductions, confirmed housing placements) — creating tradeable assets that retroactive funders can purchase to reward proven impact, aligning incentives so that programs that actually deliver attract more capital than programs that report well.
whyEthereum: Composability: impact certificates issued on a shared layer can be purchased by any retroactive funder — the same verified outcome can attract capital from government pay-for-success programs, foundation impact investments, and corporate ESG budgets without the program needing separate reporting relationships with each.

title: Cross-party inventory verification and inventory-backed lending
problem: Lenders offering inventory financing have no independent way to verify that pledged inventory actually exists, hasn't been double-pledged to another lender, and matches the borrower's representations — a $600B+ market plagued by warehouse receipt fraud (e.g., the Qingdao metals scandal where the same copper and aluminum were pledged to multiple banks simultaneously). The underlying issue is that inventory records across warehouses, suppliers, and retailers are maintained in disconnected systems with no shared source of truth.
solution: Tokenized warehouse receipts linked to verified physical inventory — with each receipt representing a specific lot in a specific warehouse, ownership transferable onchain, and double-pledging detectable by any lender checking the ledger before extending credit.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: tokenized warehouse receipts create a single ownership record that any lender can independently verify — double-pledging becomes structurally impossible because the same receipt can't be transferred to two parties simultaneously.

title: Cryptographic source protection for journalists
problem: A government whistleblower who wants to leak to a journalist faces a dilemma: submitting documents anonymously makes the story hard to verify and easy to dismiss, while providing proof of their insider status — job title, department, access level — makes them identifiable and exposes them to prosecution.
solution: Zero-knowledge systems where sources prove their organizational affiliation — that they work at a specific agency or hold a specific clearance level — without revealing their identity, generating a cryptographic attestation that journalists can publish to establish credibility without burning the source.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: zero-knowledge proofs confirm a source's credentials cryptographically — the journalist can publish verifiable proof that a source held a specific role without the proof containing any information that identifies who the source is.

title: Digital letters of credit
problem: A letter of credit — the bank guarantee that an importer will pay an exporter on delivery — still requires physical paper documents to be couriered between banks across countries, a process that takes 5–10 days and costs $500–1500 per transaction for what is fundamentally a message saying 'we guarantee payment if these conditions are met.'
solution: Smart contract-based letters of credit that issue, transfer, and settle digitally — releasing payment to the exporter automatically when shipping documents and delivery conditions are cryptographically verified, without physical document exchange.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: payment releases automatically when verifiable conditions are met cryptographically — the 5–10 day physical document courier process collapses to minutes because the document and the payment obligation are the same object.

title: Manufacturing compliance and batch traceability
problem: FDA 21 CFR Part 11 requires electronic records for pharmaceutical and medical device manufacturing to be attributable, legible, contemporaneous, original, and accurate (ALCOA) — but most manufacturers store batch records in mutable databases where a quality engineer can alter a result after the fact without detection, and reconciling records across suppliers for a single product lot requires weeks of manual document retrieval.
solution: Append-only batch records that capture every production step, quality test, and deviation with cryptographic attestation at the moment of recording — satisfying ALCOA requirements structurally rather than through policy, and enabling instant lot-level traceability across the supplier chain when a recall is triggered.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: production data is cryptographically committed at the time of recording, making post-hoc alteration detectable — regulators get an audit trail that satisfies ALCOA by construction rather than relying on SOPs that can be circumvented.

title: Spot freight market for trucking
problem: Freight brokers sit between shippers who need loads moved and carriers who have trucks available, taking 15–20% margins on every transaction in exchange for making the match — a spread that exists almost entirely because the broker holds the relationship and information asymmetry, not because they add logistical value that a transparent protocol couldn't replace.
solution: An open spot freight marketplace where shippers post loads with specifications and payment terms, carriers bid directly, payment is escrowed onchain and released automatically upon verified delivery, and both parties build a public reputation from their transaction history — cutting out the broker layer on transactions that don't require physical coordination.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: payment is escrowed and releases automatically when GPS-verified delivery is confirmed — neither party needs to trust the other or a broker to hold funds honestly, and the reputation system is portable across any platform that reads the same ledger.

title: AI model and training data markets
problem: AI models and training datasets are shared through ad-hoc channels with no enforceable licensing, usage metering, or royalty distribution — so creators lose control the moment they publish. The problem runs deeper for the creative workers whose output trained these models: writers, artists, and photographers have no mechanism to verify whether their work was included in a training corpus, no ability to opt out retroactively, and no way to claim revenue from models that directly replicate their style.
solution: Tokenized AI models and training datasets with programmable licensing terms set at registration — enabling usage metering, automatic royalty distribution to all rights holders, and cryptographic provenance records that let creators verify inclusion in training corpora and receive proportional compensation without relying on AI companies to voluntarily disclose what they trained on.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: licensing terms and compensation execute automatically when verified usage is confirmed — neither model operators nor dataset publishers can selectively report usage, and creators receive payment proportional to actual consumption without a platform intermediary.

title: Decentralized content moderation
problem: Social platforms rely on centralized moderation teams and opaque algorithms to decide what is spam, manipulation, or harmful — systems that don't scale, lack accountability, and are trivially captured by political pressure. Reputation-weighted community moderation and verified-human signals exist as concepts but have no neutral infrastructure to run on that any platform could adopt.
solution: Modular moderation infrastructure combining community-written notes on contested content, reputation-weighted flagging where established accounts carry more signal, and stake-based curation where moderators put tokens at risk on their judgments — all running on a shared layer that any platform can read without building its own moderation stack from scratch.
whyEthereum: Composability: reputation scores, stake records, and community note histories live on a shared layer — any platform can plug into the same moderation signal without rebuilding the infrastructure, and no single company controls what gets suppressed.

title: Onchain IP registration and prior art

Inventors lack a tamper-proof way to timestamp lab notebooks and experimental data, while creators have no instant, cross-jurisdictional proof of authorship that doesn't require navigating multiple national systems.
solution: Immutable, cryptographically timestamped records for both creative works and inventions — establishing proof of creation, authorship, and prior art at the moment of registration, verifiable instantly across jurisdictions without filing separately in each one.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: a cryptographic timestamp on an immutable ledger proves exactly when a work existed and who registered it — creating evidence of ownership that any court or platform can verify without a government registry.

title: Parametric weather insurance for farmers

solution: Parametric insurance products for farmers triggered automatically by satellite imagery and weather station data — when rainfall or temperature readings cross pre-agreed thresholds indicating crop loss, payouts execute without a claim form or adjuster, making it economically viable to serve smallholders at scale.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: payouts trigger automatically when oracle-verified weather data crosses a threshold — no adjuster, no claim form, no waiting period, and no dispute about whether the conditions were met.

title: Opportunity markets
problem: People with deep contextual expertise — a music fan who spots an unsigned artist, a researcher who recognizes a breakthrough paper, a local supplier who notices a successful business before investors — have valuable information but no institutional connections and no mechanism to monetize their insight. Traditional scout programs can't scale beyond the institution's ability to vet scouts and recommendations. Public prediction markets don't work either: for a scout to profit, someone must lose an offsetting amount — and no market maker wants to bet against thousands of opportunities they've never heard of. If an institution subsidizes liquidity publicly, competitors free-ride on the same signal.
solution: Private prediction markets where a sponsor (a label, VC, hiring firm) provides liquidity against outcomes like 'will we sign Artist X?' — but keeps prices visible only to themselves during an opportunity window of roughly two weeks. Anyone can create a market for any opportunity and stake their conviction. As scouts buy YES and drive prices up, the sponsor sees a rising private signal and investigates. If they act and the outcome resolves positively, the scout is paid automatically. After the window closes, positions become public. Designed by Dave White and Matt Liston (Paradigm, August 2025).
whyEthereum: Enforcement: scout payouts execute automatically via smart contract when resolution conditions are met, removing the need for sponsors to manually evaluate and honor claims. Verifiability: while prices are private during the opportunity window, all trades become publicly auditable afterward, creating reputational accountability for sponsors who might otherwise exploit their information asymmetry.

whyEthereum: Enforcement: payment releases only when oracle-verified outcomes are confirmed — funders can't be pressured to pay early, and providers can't claim payment for outcomes that weren't independently verified.

title: Participatory governance and verifiable voting
problem: Citizen participation in budgeting, voting, and policy feedback is limited to infrequent, low-trust mechanisms that provide no auditable proof that inputs were counted or acted upon. Formal elections compound the problem: existing e-voting systems ask voters to trust a proprietary black box counted their vote correctly, with no way for individual voters to verify their ballot was included without the operator confirming it.
solution: End-to-end verifiable voting using zero-knowledge proofs — letting each voter confirm their ballot was counted correctly while keeping votes secret — combined with participatory budgeting, petitions, and policy consultation platforms where every input is recorded and outcomes are publicly auditable.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: votes and budget inputs are cryptographically recorded — participants can independently verify that their contribution was counted and acted on, restoring trust in the process.

solution: Systems that give patients direct control over their medical data in a standardized portable format — enabling secure sharing across providers, granular consent over which details are disclosed, and verifiable attestation of test results and clinical findings that can't be silently altered.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: health records and test results carry cryptographic provenance that any provider can verify — patients control access, and tampering is publicly detectable.

title: Policy-as-code
problem: Statutes and regulations are published as natural-language prose that requires expensive legal interpretation, making it impossible to automatically test whether a system or process is compliant before deployment. Enforcement compounds the problem: even where rules are clear, triggering consequences — traffic restrictions, fines, subsidy releases — requires human sign-off at each step, meaning enforcement reliably lags the events it's supposed to address.
solution: Machine-readable statutes and regulations with testable compliance logic — so businesses can verify conformance before deployment and enforcement responses execute automatically when oracle-verified conditions are met. Air quality policy is an immediate application: when sensor feeds cross legal thresholds, traffic restrictions and fines trigger without a human decision at each step.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: machine-readable regulations can be directly referenced by smart contracts — compliance becomes testable before the fact and enforcement executes automatically, making the gap between a rule and its consequence a protocol property rather than a bureaucratic one.

title: Portable credentials and professional identity
problem: Academic credentials are issued as PDFs locked in institutional databases, skills acquired across employers and platforms exist in disconnected silos, and workers lose their ratings and reputation every time they switch platforms. NGO workers and journalists face the same problem in higher-stakes form: they must repeatedly prove affiliations in hostile environments while sharing full identity documents puts them at risk of surveillance and retaliation.
solution: Tamper-proof, portable credentials — diplomas, micro-credentials, work history, ratings, union membership, press credentials — that workers and professionals own and carry across platforms, employers, and jurisdictions, with selective disclosure so only the relevant claim is shared in each context.
whyEthereum: Composability: credentials issued by any institution on a shared layer are readable by any platform or verifier without bilateral agreements — a journalist's press credential, a nurse's license, and a freelancer's rating history all live in the same portable wallet.

title: Clinical trial pre-registration and results registry
problem: Pharmaceutical companies register clinical trials, then selectively publish only the results that support their drug — a practice called outcome reporting bias that systematically distorts the medical evidence base and leads doctors to prescribe treatments that don't work or cause harm, because negative results simply disappear.
solution: An immutable registry where trial protocols and primary endpoints are cryptographically committed before a trial begins — making it publicly provable whether published results match what was pre-registered and impossible to quietly bury unfavorable outcomes.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: cryptographic timestamps prove exactly what was pre-registered and when — selective reporting becomes publicly detectable rather than just ethically discouraged, because the original commitment cannot be altered retroactively.

title: Prediction markets for scientific claims
problem: Research funding committees allocate billions based on grant applications reviewed by small expert panels, with no mechanism to aggregate what the broader scientific community actually believes about which hypotheses are likely to replicate, which drugs will fail trials, or which research directions will prove fruitful — leaving funding systematically biased toward incremental work from established labs.
solution: Open prediction markets where researchers stake tokens on the outcomes of specific scientific claims — will this trial replicate, will this drug hit its primary endpoint — with payouts flowing to those who were right. Market prices surface collective scientific belief in real time, and accumulated winnings serve as a credibility signal for funding allocation.
whyEthereum: Enforcement: payouts execute automatically when oracle-verified outcomes are confirmed — participants can't manipulate settlement, and the market's track record is publicly auditable, making it a credible input to funding decisions.

whyEthereum: Enforcement: payouts execute automatically when attested medical events match pre-defined trigger conditions — eliminating the manual claims process for product types where the trigger is binary and verifiable, while leaving complex claims to traditional adjudication.

title: Proof of personhood
problem: Any digital system that gives one vote, one reward, or one allocation per person is immediately gamed by whoever can spin up the most fake accounts — airdrops get drained by bots, governance votes get bought in bulk, and social platforms are flooded with coordinated inauthentic behavior — because there is no cheap, privacy-preserving way to prove an account belongs to a unique real human.
solution: Systems that establish a cryptographic guarantee that an account belongs to a unique living person — without revealing who that person is — enabling sybil-resistant token distributions, one-person-one-vote governance, and spam-free social platforms.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: personhood attestations are cryptographically issued and publicly checkable by any application — platforms verify uniqueness without storing biometric data or trusting a single identity provider that becomes a centralization point.

title: Migrant worker remittances
problem: A Kenyan nurse working in London sending $200 home loses $12–20 to Western Union and correspondent bank fees — more than the entire daily wage of the family member receiving it — while the transfer takes 1–3 days and requires both parties to have access to physical agents.
solution: Stablecoin transfers that move value internationally in minutes at near-zero marginal cost, with recipients cashing out through local mobile money networks or holding value in digital wallets without needing a bank account.
whyEthereum: Composability: stablecoins settle on a shared global layer without correspondent banking infrastructure — sender and recipient transact directly regardless of jurisdiction, with no intermediary banks extracting spread on each hop.

title: Verifiable dataset provenance and reuse tracking for research
problem: Researchers who create high-quality datasets — curating, cleaning, and annotating data that takes months of work — receive almost no academic credit when other researchers use their data, because there's no reliable mechanism to track dataset reuse across papers and institutions. Simultaneously, researchers who want to build on prior work often can't verify whether a dataset has been altered since publication or confirm they're working with the same version that produced the original findings.
solution: A registry where published datasets are cryptographically fingerprinted at the time of deposit, with every subsequent access and citation recorded — so dataset creators receive verifiable credit proportional to actual reuse, and downstream researchers can confirm they're working with an unaltered version of the original data.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: cryptographic fingerprints prove dataset integrity since publication, and onchain access records create an unfalsifiable citation trail — dataset creators get credit for actual reuse and downstream researchers can confirm data hasn't been tampered with.

title: Digital product passports
problem: The EU's Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation (ESPR) mandates digital product passports for most goods sold in Europe by 2030 — but no neutral infrastructure exists for brands, recyclers, and regulators across supply chains to read and write to a shared product record. Without it, origin and certification claims rely on paper trails that can be forged at every handoff, recycled materials lose their provenance once they re-enter supply chains, and secondhand buyers have no trustworthy way to verify repair history or remaining lifecycle value.
solution: Append-only product records that persist through every ownership transfer and material handoff — tracking origin, certifications, condition, repair history, and end-of-life status in a format any manufacturer, recycler, regulator, or secondhand buyer can read, satisfying ESPR requirements while making circular economy claims cryptographically verifiable rather than self-reported.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: every certification, ownership transfer, and material handoff is recorded as an immutable onchain event — fraud and greenwashing become publicly detectable, and the same product record is readable by any party in the supply chain without bilateral data-sharing agreements.

solution: Onchain proofs that a specific model produced a specific output, enabling accountability and auditability
whyEthereum: Verifiability: cryptographic proofs allow anyone to confirm which model version produced a given output, creating an auditable trail that holds providers accountable for hallucinations or manipulation.

title: Water rights trading and usage verification
problem: In drought-prone regions, water rights are traded in informal bilateral markets with no price transparency, while actual usage is monitored by infrequent physical inspections that miss illegal diversions — creating a tragedy of the commons where overuse is profitable and nearly undetectable until aquifers are depleted.
solution: Tokenized water rights tradeable on transparent markets, with IoT-verified usage continuously recorded onchain — creating a real-time allocation registry where overuse is immediately visible and rights can be transferred without paper title processes.
whyEthereum: Verifiability: actual water usage attested by IoT sensors is recorded immutably — illegal diversions become publicly detectable as deviations from onchain allocations, rather than discovered only when downstream users notice reduced flow.

title: Wearables and health data exchanges

solution: Platforms that let individuals share fitness and biometric data and receive compensation or benefits in return
whyEthereum: Composability: individuals connect their data to any research program or insurance product that accepts the standard — no platform lock-in, and users capture value from their own data.
